message:
fix(runtime): make asx_runtime_reset clear all global diagnostic state

Previously asx_runtime_reset only cleared lifecycle arenas and ghost
monitors, leaving scheduler events, parallel workers, channels, timers,
trace ring, telemetry counters, hindsight buffer, adapters, fault
injection, and the new event log in stale state between test runs.

This caused subtle cross-test pollution: a timer registered in one test
could fire in the next, telemetry tier persisted across resets, and
channel queues retained phantom messages.

Now reset calls every subsystem's reset function in order:
  asx_scheduler_event_reset, asx_parallel_reset, asx_channel_reset,
  asx_timer_wheel_reset, asx_trace_reset, asx_telemetry_reset,
  asx_hindsight_reset, asx_adapter_reset_all, asx_fault_clear,
  asx_event_log_reset, asx_error_ledger_reset

The header doc-comment is updated to list the cleared domains, and
test_trace.c gains a dedicated test that exercises and verifies each
subsystem is actually zeroed after reset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
